content.problemStatement:
Chef has a combination lock with NNN wheels (numbered 111 through NNN). For each valid iii, on the iii-th wheel, the integers from 000 to AiA_iAi​ (inclusive) are written in ascending order (000 and AiA_iAi​ are also adjacent). Initially, one value on each wheel is selected uniformly randomly and independently.

The lock operates in a very peculiar way: it opens when there is at least one wheel where either 000 or the largest value on that wheel is selected. Chef wants to open the lock using the smallest possible number of operations; in one operation, he may choose one wheel and rotate it clockwise or counterclockwise by 111 unit (i.e. select a value that is adjacent to the previously selected value on that wheel).

Find the expected value of the number of operations which Chef needs to perform to open the lock.

The expected number of operations can be represented as a fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where PPP is a non-negative integer and QQQ a positive integer coprime with 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353. You should calculate P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353, where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 denotes the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
